tasks.title: Add Elasticsearch to Docker Compose and define dependencies
tasks.description: Configure Elasticsearch 8.x as a single-node service in docker-compose.yml and update the backend service to depend on it.
tasks.details: Update `docker-compose.yml` to include a service named `elasticsearch` using the `elasticsearch:8.x` image. Set environment variables: `discovery.type=single-node`, `xpack.security.enabled=false`, and `ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m -Xmx512m`. Map port 9200. Define a named volume `esdata` for persistence. Add a healthcheck to ensure the cluster is 'green' or 'yellow'. Update the `backend` service to include `depends_on: elasticsearch: condition: service_healthy`.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `docker compose up -d` and verify the elasticsearch container starts and passes health checks. Curl `http://localhost:9200` to confirm it responds with the cluster info.

tasks.title: Update configuration and install dependencies
tasks.description: Add Elasticsearch settings to the application configuration and install the official Python client.
tasks.details: 1. Add `elasticsearch` to `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`. 2. Update `app/core/config.py` to include `ElasticsearchSettings` with `url: str = 'http://elasticsearch:9200'` and `index_name: str = 'cards'`. 3. Export `elasticsearch_settings` from the config module. 4. Update `.env.example` with the new environment variables.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pip install` (or equivalent) and verify the package is available. Run `pytest` or a small script to verify `app/core/config.py` correctly loads values from environment variables.

tasks.title: Implement Elasticsearch client management and lifespan hooks
tasks.description: Create a centralized Elasticsearch client and ensure it connects/disconnects during the FastAPI app lifecycle.
tasks.details: Create `app/core/elasticsearch.py`. Implement an `AsyncElasticsearch` client factory. In `app/main.py` (or where the lifespan is defined), initialize the client during startup and close it during shutdown. Store the client instance in `app.state.es`. Follow the pattern used for MongoDB if applicable.
tasks.testStrategy: Start the FastAPI application and check logs for successful connection to Elasticsearch. Verify that no connection leak errors occur on shutdown.

tasks.title: Define Elasticsearch index mapping and initialization
tasks.description: Create the 'cards' index with specific mappings for fuzzy search and structured filtering on startup.
tasks.details: Define a mapping in `app/core/elasticsearch.py`: `name` as `text` (standard analyzer), `cmc` as `float`, `set` as `keyword`, and `released_at` as `date`. Ensure other ScryfallCard fields are indexed (as keyword or text where appropriate). Implement a function `init_elasticsearch()` that checks if the index exists and creates it with the mapping if not. Call this in the app startup lifespan.
tasks.testStrategy: Delete the index manually if it exists, restart the app, and use `GET /cards/_mapping` via curl to verify the mapping was applied correctly.

tasks.title: Implement Card Indexing Service
tasks.description: Create a service to handle bulk indexing and individual upserts of card data into Elasticsearch.
tasks.details: Create `app/services/card_indexer.py`. Use `elasticsearch.helpers.async_bulk`. Ensure the document `_id` matches the Scryfall `id` for idempotent upserts. Map the `ScryfallCard` Pydantic model to a dictionary suitable for ES. Log errors using `loguru` without failing the entire batch. Implementation: `async def index_cards(cards: List[ScryfallCard]): ...`.
tasks.testStrategy: Write a unit test that mocks the ES client and verifies that `async_bulk` is called with the correctly formatted document list and ID mapping.

tasks.title: Remove Elasticsearch sync from the ingestion pipeline
tasks.description: Modify the existing MongoDB card ingestion logic to remove automatic Elasticsearch indexing.
tasks.details: Locate the ingestion logic in `app/data_pipeline/ingestion/json_records.py` and the route in `app/api/routes/ingest.py`. Remove calls to `index_cards` and the dependency on the Elasticsearch client in the ingestion pipeline. Ensure `POST /data-pipeline/ingestion/json-records` only affects the MongoDB collection.
tasks.testStrategy: Trigger a card ingestion via the API. Verify that records are added to MongoDB but NO new records appear in Elasticsearch `GET /cards/_count`.

tasks.title: Develop Elasticsearch Query Builder
tasks.description: Create a utility to translate API search parameters into an Elasticsearch DSL query.
tasks.details: In `app/core/card_search.py`, implement `build_card_query(params: CardSearchParams)`. Use a `bool` query. Add a `must` match clause for `name` with `fuzziness: 'AUTO'`. Add `filter` clauses for `cmc` (term), `set` (term), and `released_at` (range: gte/lte). Handle pagination using `from` (offset) and `size`. Return a dict ready for `es.search()`.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests in `tests/core/test_card_search.py`. Pass various param combinations (only name, name+cmc, name+date_range) and assert the resulting dictionary matches the expected ES DSL structure.

tasks.title: Define Search API Models
tasks.description: Create Pydantic models for search request parameters and paginated responses.
tasks.details: In `app/models/api.py` (or similar), create `CardSearchParams` (Query, CMC, Set, released_at_from/to, page, page_size) and `CardSearchResponse` (items: List[ScryfallCard], total: int, page: int, page_size: int). Ensure proper validation (e.g., page_size max 100).
tasks.testStrategy: Validate the models with various valid and invalid inputs using Pydantic's `model_validate`. Ensure type hinting is correct for the API docs.

tasks.title: Implement GET /cards/search endpoint
tasks.description: Create the FastAPI route to handle structured card search requests.
tasks.details: Create `app/api/routes/card_search.py`. Define `GET /` (mounted at `/cards/search`). Inject the ES client. Use the query builder from Task 7. Execute the search. Parse the `_source` of hits back into `ScryfallCard` objects. Return a `CardSearchResponse`. Register the router in `app/api/main.py`.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual testing via Swagger UI (`/docs`). Verify that searching for a card name returns results, and applying filters (like `set`) correctly narrows them down.

tasks.title: Implement POST /cards/search/index endpoint
tasks.description: Create a dedicated endpoint for indexing cards directly into Elasticsearch.
tasks.details: In `app/api/routes/card_search.py`, add a `POST /index` endpoint. It should accept an `UploadFile` (JSON dataset). Reuse the logic from `app/services/card_indexer.py` to bulk-index the cards into the `cards` index in ES. This endpoint should NOT touch MongoDB.
tasks.testStrategy: Post a JSON file to `/cards/search/index`. Verify that cards are searchable via `GET /cards/search` but are not present in MongoDB (if they weren't there already).

tasks.title: Integration Testing for Search Workflow
tasks.description: End-to-end testing of the separated ingestion and search flows.
tasks.details: Update `tests/api/routes/test_card_search.py`. 1. Test `POST /data-pipeline/ingestion/json-records` and verify MongoDB update ONLY. 2. Test `POST /cards/search/index` and verify ES update ONLY. 3. Perform various searches to ensure the indexed data is queryable.

metadata.description: Separated ES indexing from MongoDB ingestion
